ab: mio yonta emoo ’o ba’i?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: ’a mio, mita yonto hopo ho oengʉtʉ.
en: Yes, she’s sleeping in her room.

ab: mio yonta emoo ’o ba’i?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: o’amos’a, mita emoyafo, tena maine’e ho yofna.
ch: B:不在，她剛出去了，要晚上才回家。
en: No, she’s not. She just went out and will be back in the evening.

ab: mio yonta emoo ’o ak’i?
en: Is grandfather at home?

ab: ’a mita yone nikai, naho eno usa ho i’eima.
en: He’s upstairs. You can go up and find him.

ab: pano mo yonta emoo?
en: Is anyone at home?

ab: ’iama yapasuyongʉ, noana’va o’te aiti na suu, leako ʉm’ʉmnʉ?
ch: B:是yapasuyongʉ阿，很久沒有看到你了，你好嗎?
en: It’s yapasuyongʉ. I haven’t seen you in a while! How have you been?

ab: ’a lea’u na’no ʉm’ʉmnʉ! os’o i’eima suu ho toa ya’ei.
en: I’m doing great! I came to chat with you.

ab: mo yonenu ’o amosu?
en: Where is your father?

ab: mita yonto ’oyona pei’i ho cmuecuyu to chae, te’o eno usa ho yooho’a.
ch: B:他在廚房炒菜，我去叫他。
en: He’s in the kitchen cooking. Let me go call him.

ab: mo uhnenu ’o paicʉ?
en: Where did paicʉ go?

ab: ’a mita uhto emoo no teo’ua ho tʉtpʉtʉ to teo’ua.
en: She went to the chicken coop to catch chickens.

ab: ak’i, mimza cu esmi.
en: Grandfather, we’re here.

ab: o’amos’a yonta emoo ’o ak’i, mo yone ezoyʉ,, temu yonta emoo ho totea.
ch: B:你們的爺爺不在家，他在田裡，你們在家等他回來吧。
en: Your grandfather is not at home. He’s in the field. Wait for him to come back.

ab: ma miko uhtan’e amoconi!
en: Uncle, you’re here!

ab: noana’va o’te aiti, ma mikocu yubankake.
en: I haven’t seen you in a while. You’ve grown taller.

ab: na’no kokaekaebʉ ho os’o aiti na suu ho miko cmʉ’ho.
en: It’s so good to see you.

ab: na eni te’o faeni muu ci ’ungeai, te siya no tvoyuhoa.
ch: B:這是給你們的鳳梨，要放到冰箱裡。
en: Here’s a pineapple for you. Put it in the refrigerator.

ab: miko cu noana’o o’te uhtan’e, te’o eho’ʉ ho toehungu bonʉ.
ch: A:你這麼久沒來，我邀請大家一起來吃飯。
en: It’s been a while. Let’s have a meal together.

ab: o’ate ’ahʉyʉ, te’oc’o mimo no ’ocea, teto uhto aemana ho yoya’ei.
ch: B:不用麻煩了，我喝茶就好，我們來客廳聊天。
en: No need to bother. I’ll just have tea. Let’s sit in the living room and chat.

ab: ’aveoveoyʉ ho iko aʉt’ʉt’ʉca, te’o cu maine’e.
ch: A:謝謝你的招待，我要回去了。
en: Thank you for your hospitality. I have to go now.

ab: isin’a ala chua si teo’ua, teko hafa maine’e ho oupopoha’va.
ch: B:這隻雞剛殺好，你帶回去慢慢吃。
en: This chicken was just freshly killed. Take it home and enjoy it slowly.
